subsection.chapeau: A person shall be fined under title 18, imprisoned for not more than 2 years, or both, if the person with the intent to defraud—

paragraph.content: provides false information to the Secretary of Transportation or a State issuing authority regarding the identification of a vessel under this chapter; or
tampers with, removes, or falsifies the unique vessel identification number assigned to a vessel under section 12502 of this title.

subsection.chapeau: A person is liable to the United States Government for a civil penalty of not more than $10,000 if the person—

paragraph.content: provides false information to the Secretary or a State issuing authority regarding the identification of a vessel under this chapter;
violates section 12502 of this title; or
fails to comply with requirements prescribed by the Secretary under section 12505 of this title.

subsection.content: A vessel involved in a violation of this chapter, or regulation under this chapter, and its equipment, may be seized by, and forfeited to, the Government.
If a person, not an individual, is involved in a violation of this chapter, the president or chief executive of the person also is subject to any penalty provided under this section.

note.heading: Historical and Revision Notes
note.p: This section establishes criminal and civil penalties for certain violations of chapter 125 of title 46 (as enacted by this Act).

note.heading: Effective Date
note.p: Section effective Jan. 1, 1989, with certain exceptions and qualifications, see section 107 of Pub. L. 100–710, set out as a note under section 31301 of this title.
